raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "illustrating the mechanism and consequences of physician-induced autosuggestion to advocate for optimistic framing in medical communication",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "a doctor's title confers automatic suggestive authority over patients",
    "verbal declarations of incurability reliably trigger harmful autosuggestion",
    "verbal declarations of potential curability reliably produce beneficial clinical outcomes via autosuggestion",
    "the difference in outcomes is attributable primarily to the physician's wording rather than other factors"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as a contrastive example; the causal mechanism is asserted without qualification or counterexamples",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the audience accepts autosuggestion as a dominant therapeutic factor and the statement is used to train clinicians in calibrated positive communication",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the statement is treated as empirical proof that optimistic pronouncements can override disease progression regardless of medical evidence or patient condition"
}
